ConvertKit vs Beehiiv: Which Is Better?

Choosing between ConvertKit and Beehiiv? Here's a side-by-side comparison to help you decide.

Quick verdict: Choose ConvertKit if you need creators or writers. Choose Beehiiv if you prioritize writers or newsletter-creators.

ConvertKit

PricingFree / $29/mo Creator / $59/mo Creator Pro
Rating★★★★ 4.4/5

Key Features

  • Tag-based subscriber management and segmentation
  • Landing page and signup form builder
  • Email sequences for automated drip campaigns
  • Creator Network for cross-promotion with other creators
  • Digital product sales and delivery built-in
  • Subscriber scoring and engagement tracking

Pros

  • Tag-based subscriber management is intuitive and powerful; segment your audience without complex automations
  • Landing page builder is simple but effective; you can go from zero to a signup page in minutes
  • Email sequences are straightforward to set up and ConvertKit handles timing, tagging, and analytics
  • Creator Network feature helps grow your audience by cross-promoting with other creators
  • Integrates with all the tools creators use (Gumroad, Teachable, WordPress, Zapier)

Cons

  • Expensive — $29/mo for the Creator plan jumps quickly from the free tier's 1,000 subscribers
  • Email editing is basic; the visual editor is serviceable but not as flexible as Mailchimp or ActiveCampaign
  • No built-in CRM features; it's purely email marketing, not a full marketing platform
  • Automation capabilities are limited compared to dedicated marketing automation tools
  • Template library is minimal; you'll need to design your own emails or use a third-party template
Try ConvertKit →

Beehiiv

PricingFree / $42/mo Grow / $84/mo Scale
Rating★★★★ 4.3/5

Key Features

  • Newsletter publishing with built-in website and RSS
  • Boom referral program for subscriber-driven growth
  • Ad network for newsletter monetization
  • Recommendation algorithm for newsletter discovery
  • Advanced analytics including growth sources and revenue attribution
  • Segmentation and automated email sequences

Pros

  • Boom (referral program) turns your subscribers into growth engines by gamifying sharing
  • Built-in ad network lets you monetize without chasing sponsors yourself
  • Recommendation algorithm (like Substack's but better) helps new readers discover your newsletter
  • Website builder turns your newsletter into a full publication site, not just an email
  • Analytics are comprehensive — open rates, click rates, growth sources, and revenue breakdowns

Cons

  • Gets expensive fast — the Grow plan ($42/mo) is where most useful features live
  • Free tier limits you to 2,500 subscribers and lacks core features like custom domains and advanced analytics
  • The platform has changed pricing multiple times, which creates trust concerns for long-term planning
  • Editor is functional but not as polished as ConvertKit's or Substack's writing experience
  • Migration tools from other platforms are basic; don't expect a seamless transition from Substack
Try Beehiiv →

Which Should You Choose?

Both ConvertKit and Beehiiv are excellent tools in their category. The right choice depends on your specific needs:

  • Choose ConvertKit if: ConvertKit is the email marketing platform built specifically for creators. Unlike Mailchimp which tries to be everything for everyone, ConvertKit focuses on what creators need: landing pages, email sequences, tags, and subscriber management that actually makes sense. It's not the cheapest option, but the simplicity and creator-focused features justify the premium for many.
  • Choose Beehiiv if: Beehiiv is the newsletter platform that turned the Substack model on its head by giving creators way more tools — a built-in website, ad network, referral program, and reCOMMENDED algorithm for discovery. It's eating Substack's lunch by offering everything Substack does plus actual growth features. If you're starting a newsletter in 2025, Beehiiv is the default choice.
We may earn a commission if you purchase through links. All opinions are our own.
